channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: I’m a tech pro and these are my secrets to better flights and luxury travel
item.description: Here are some travel hacks to save money on your next vacation including setting up price alerts, remaining flexible and checking for 24-hour price drops.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Intel hires former board member as new CEO in struggling chipmaker's latest comeback attempt
item.description: Struggling chipmaker Intel has hired former board member and semiconductor industry veteran Lip-Bu Tan as the latest in a succession of CEOs to attempt to turn around the company that helped define Silicon Valley.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A volcano near Alaska's largest city could erupt in the coming weeks or months, scientists say
item.description: A volcano near Alaska’s largest city is showing new signs of unrest, with experts saying the likelihood of an eruption at Mount Spurr in the next few weeks or months has increased.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Apple Blocks Opponents in India from Accessing Confidential Information
item.description: India's Competition Commission denies Apple's opponents access to confidential information, blocking their ability to review antitrust findings against the US firm.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: At long last, OpenStack (now known as OpenInfra Foundation) joins Linux Foundation
item.description: After more than a decade as an independent open-source foundation, the OpenStack project is joining the Linux Foundation in a move aimed at accelerating collaboration across the open infrastructure ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple faces UK antitrust threat as regulator targets browsers
item.description: Apple faces a renewed threat in the UK, where a new report from the top antitrust regulator says both it and Google are holding back innovation in the mobile browser market. By far, most of the criticism is being leveled at Apple.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Satellite Revolution: How AI-Powered Satellites are Changing Disaster Response Forever
item.description: NASA and Ubotica's Dynamic Targeting system is revolutionizing satellite operations in space, aiding real-time image processing and autonomous decision-making.
item.dc:creator: Victor Dey, Fast Company

item.title: The LG C5 is now available to pre-order in the UK (mostly), and the price is right
item.description: UK pricing has finally arrived for the sure-to-be popular LG C5, and it's identical to its predecessor's launch prices.
item.dc:creator: james.davidson@futurenet.com (James Davidson), TechRadar

item.title: More than 10,000 domains registered for smishing campaign: Report
item.description: Cybercriminals have registered more than 10,000 domains for various smishing scams, according to a recent report from the threat intelligence arm at cybersecurity firm Palo Alto Networks.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: FTC asks judge to delay Amazon trial due to resource constraints
item.description: A lawyer for the Federal Trade Commission says the agency needs to seek a trial delay in a case against Amazon due to staffing and budgetary challenges.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Discovery+ just got a big update to its streaming app that makes it more like Max – here are 5 great new features to try
item.description: Warner Bros. Discovery has made the Discovery+ streaming app more like Max, and has introduced five great new features as part of the update.
item.dc:creator: amelia.schwanke@futurenet.com (Amelia Schwanke), TechRadar

item.title: How the Pittsburgh Steelers leverage the Fortinet Security Fabric for industry-leading defense
item.description: The Pittsburgh Steelers are one of the most recognizable American football teams, with tens of millions of fans tuning into games and a busy stadium that can hold more than 68,000 fans.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Supercharging your cybersecurity strategy with AI
item.description: Artificial intelligence has been unleashed on the world. While its power is undeniable, its full potential to wreak havoc is still unknown. Cyber-criminals have few concerns about AI running amok, because chaos furthers their goals.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Disney+ is making Andor free to stream on YouTube, and now you have no excuse not to watch the best Star Wars show
item.description: Disney+ doesn’t want you to miss out on streaming Andor before its season 2 premiere – here’s where you can watch the hit Star Wars show for free.
item.dc:creator: amelia.schwanke@futurenet.com (Amelia Schwanke), TechRadar

item.title: ChatGPT wants to write your next novel, and readers and writers alike should be very worried
item.description: Sam Altman shows off a new ChatGPT model that's very good at creative writing, as long as you want robot-written fiction.
item.dc:creator: john-anthony.disotto@futurenet.com (John-Anthony Disotto), TechRadar

item.title: New US CIO appointments, March 2025
item.description: Movers & Shakers is where you can keep up with new CIO appointments and gain valuable insight into the job market and CIO hiring trends.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta executives called ‘Careless People’ in new book from former Facebook employee-turned-whistleblower
item.description: Meta is once again facing allegations from a former employee that a growth-at-all-costs culture has caused offline harm.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Trump floats labeling violence against Tesla dealerships as domestic terrorism
item.description: President Trump hinted he would be open to labeling individuals who carry out violence at Tesla dealerships as “domestic terrorists” following a string of violent demonstrations at the electric vehicle company’s showrooms across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Finally: some truth serum for lying genAI chatbots
item.description: Ever since OpenAI made ChatGPT available to the public in late 2022, the large language model (LLM)-based generative AI (genAI) revolution has advanced at an astonishing pace. Two years and four months ago, we had only ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nebraska Legislature approves measure classifying gig economy workers as independent contractors
item.description: The Nebraska Legislature has approved a measure classifying some gig economy workers as independent contractors, according to its website. L.B. 229, introduced by state Sen. Bob Hallstrom, was approved Monday, with 33 voting in favor and 15 opposed.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: How to deliver more secure capabilities to the warfighter, faster
item.description: Rethinking the delivery process for new capabilities is essential to put cybersecurity and the warfighter front and center. The post How to deliver more secure capabilities to the warfighter, faster first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Is that iPhone app spying? Apple’s App Privacy Report reveals all
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson urges you to use Apple’s App Privacy Report to see what your apps are really up to.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Driving best value pricing – Let’s go for a ride
item.description: This blog will focus on requirements in the context of the federal government. Sound requirements drive best value pricing and performance for customer agencies The post Driving best value pricing – Let’s go for a ride first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — The Albanian Cabinet decided on Thursday to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the popular video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: HPE cuts 2,500 jobs, remains committed to Juniper buy, faces tariff issues
item.description: After sharing a mostly positive revenue report for the first quarter of its 2025 fiscal year, HPE executives detailed a number of challenges the company will face in the coming months, including layoffs, a court case over its proposed buy of Juniper Networks, and the U.S. government’s tariff plan.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Achieving unified visibility for effective zero trust implementation
item.description: All components of a zero trust architecture must generate data that is observable, available and able to be correlated within a unified data plane. The post Achieving unified visibility for effective zero trust implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walk through your future home or business space before construction starts
item.description: Lifesize Plans offers immersive walk-throughs that allow customers the opportunity to tour a home's design before construction actually begins.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
